policy_note: Default policy is 90% line coverage per source file. After the larql-compute-metal extraction (ADR-019), this crate is CPU-side only — the trait, the CpuBackend impl, pipeline types, and env-option helpers. Every file is expected to clear the 90% floor without `exclude_globs` hacks. GPU-shader coverage lives in `crates/larql-compute-metal/coverage-policy.json`. 2026-06-06: the two inherited debt baselines (`attention/decode.rs` 55%, `kv_dispatch/cpu.rs` 88%) were retired — `attention/decode.rs` is now 92.3% and `kv_dispatch/cpu.rs` 97.1%. The q4k-direct decode path is driven directly via the `Q4kFixtureIndex` fixture (`run_attention_block_decode_step_q4k_direct`, no env var needed) plus an env-gated `attention_step` test (LARQL_Q4K_DIRECT_ATTN, OnceLock-owned by that single test). Residual misses in decode.rs are attention-bias / QK-norm / v-norm branches absent from the gemma3/tinymodel Q4K fixtures (no fixture carries attn biases); residual misses in cpu.rs are the indirect-matvec `coarse_decode_step{,_with_state}` arms, which need an index that supports cached-decode but NOT direct-matvec (structurally hard — both consume the same Q4_K slices).
